name: owned_endpoints_shared_across_containers
code:
proof fn phi_2_owned_endpoints_shared_across_containers(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    c1: ContainerPtr,
    c2: ContainerPtr,
    ep: EndpointPtr,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c1),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c2),
        c1 != c2,
        container_perms[c1].value().owned_endpoints@.contains(ep),
        container_perms[c2].value().owned_endpoints@.contains(ep),
    ensures
        container_perms[c1].value().owned_endpoints@.contains(ep)
        && container_perms[c2].value().owned_endpoints@.contains(ep),
{
}
reason: No spec enforces disjointness of owned_endpoints across containers — the same endpoint can be owned by two containers simultaneously, violating the unique ownership intent described in comments

code:
proof fn phi_4_scheduler_contains_non_owned_thread(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    c_ptr: ContainerPtr,
    t: ThreadPtr,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c_ptr),
        container_perms[c_ptr].value().scheduler.wf(),
        container_perms[c_ptr].value().scheduler@.contains(t),
        !container_perms[c_ptr].value().owned_threads@.contains(t),
    ensures
        container_perms[c_ptr].value().scheduler@.contains(t),
{
}
reason: No spec requires scheduler threads to be a subset of owned_threads — a container's scheduler can schedule threads it doesn't own, breaking containment

proof fn phi_5_children_cross_container_overlap(
    root_container: ContainerPtr,
    container_perms: Map<ContainerPtr, PointsTo<Container>>,
    c1: ContainerPtr,
    c2: ContainerPtr,
    child: ContainerPtr,
)
    requires
        container_perms_wf(container_perms),
        container_tree_wf(root_container, container_perms),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c1),
        container_perms.dom().contains(c2),
        c1 != c2,
        container_perms[c1].value().children@.contains(child),
    ensures
        !container_perms[c2].value().children@.contains(child),
{
}
reason: container_childern_parent_wf requires each child's parent to equal its containing node — if child is in c1's children, its parent must be c1, so it cannot also be in c2's children (this tests whether the spec correctly enforces single-parent)
